content:
You can be stylish and a programmer, but you are exceptional then

5 min read
Just now

--

Press enter or click to view image in full size

Photo by cottonbro studio: https://www.pexels.com/photo/a-programmer-working-at-the-office-6803522/I believe people’s personalities can change from time to time depending on their surroundings, work, or life pressure, depression, mental health, etc.
Someone can be extroverted, but depending on the situation, they can be introverted and vice versa. However, the change is not prominent.
Most of my programmer friends and people have some common traits. I wouldn’t say those are good or bad. I am no one to judge. But this article is totally based on my observation and point of view about programmers who commonly behave like this.
1. Subtle Asperger’s trait
Maybe it’s just me, but I feel like most great programmers have some Asperger’s traits. More specifically, they are terrible at intuitively understanding what is going on in minds other than their own.
They have little tolerance for why others do not think the way they do and tend to blame things on user stupidity rather than their counterintuitive designs.
But those programmers who work hard to overcome this handicap by forming mental models of how people think can develop a better understanding of cognition than “normal” humans.
